label: false-dup
a: Cached user profiles expire after 5 minutes (time-based TTL). After 5 minutes, the next read fetches fresh data from the database and repopulates the cache.
b: Cache entries for user profiles are immediately invalidated (deleted from Redis) on any profile update. The next read after an update always goes to the database. This is write-through invalidation, separate from the TTL policy.
reviewer_notes: Two cache invalidation mechanisms layered together: A is time-based TTL; B is write-through invalidation on update. Complementary, not the same fact.
